title: pulsatilla alpina subsp. cantabrica
intro: Pulsatilla alpina subsp. cantabrica, also known as the Cantabrian pasque flower, is a subspecies of the pasque flower plant, native to mountainous regions in northern Spain. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Pulsatilla alpina subsp. cantabrica include relief from menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a salve or ointment.
